pattern: Greedily peeling off the earliest window that contains all k faces shows how many full lengths of arbitrary sequences are realizable, and the answer is that count plus one.
hints: Think about how many times you can scan forward and collect a complete set of all k distinct faces.
Each complete set you greedily consume guarantees every sequence of that length can be embedded as a subsequence.
Count the number of disjoint prefixes that each first complete the full set {1..k}; the shortest impossible length is that count plus one.

edge_inputs.description: a face never appears so length 1 is impossible
k equals 1 with at least one roll
all faces present exactly once gives one set
missing one face entirely despite many rolls

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(1, 50)
    n = max(1, size)
    rolls = [rng.randint(1, k) for _ in range(n)]
    return [rolls, k]
stress.note: varied k and length exercises set-completion counting
